note: BRANCH-HYGIENE + LIFECYCLE ANOMALY, recorded rather than silently resolved. (1) Branch test-framework-rebuild carries THREE CRs gated together by owner decision, so it is not one independently revertable unit; per-CR attribution of the 64-file freeze footprint is therefore approximate where the CRs overlap (the backfill -> scaffold identifier renames are recorded in BOTH spec-organization-rebuild's and test-framework-rebuild's Clearance ledgers). (2) The root spec.md was never re-opened implemented -> draft when these behavior-changing CRs started, so this gate records implemented -> approved, a transition the lifecycle state machine does not list. The end state is what the gate's approve verb specifies; the missing re-open is a gap in the recorded history, not a fresh decision.
